rulings.Case Title: CARLOS GAYTAN SR. VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL CO.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant CalPortland Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its burden to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim by demonstrating that plaintiff does not have an asbestos-related illness or injury. Dr. Bruch¿s report and declaration and Dr. Powers¿ report create a triable issue whether plaintiff has an asbestos-related illness or injury. As to the fear of cancer argument, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden on this issue. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff has a claim for fear of cancer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KENTON SHAIFFER et al VS. DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication (negligent design defect, strict liability design defect, negligent failure to warn, strict liability failure to warn, and loss of consortium) are denied. As to the so-called OEM and asbestos content issues, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Shaiffer¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to substantial factor causation, defendant sustained its burden and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Shaiffer¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. As to loss of consortium, defendant sustained its burden and Mrs. Shaiffer¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable under such cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
